title: Draba cantabriae
intro: Draba cantabriae, also known as Cantabrian whitlowgrass, is a rare, alpine plant species native to the Cantabrian Mountains in northern Spain. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing pain and oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Draba cantabriae include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and glucosinolates, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Draba cantabriae include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health issues, including arthritis and digestive problems.
taxonomy: Draba cantabriae is classified within the family Brassicaceae. This family is commonly known as the mustard or cabbage family due to its diverse species including various types of mustard, cabbage, and broccoli. Draba cantabriae is further classified in the genus Draba, which consists of several species of small, flowering plants. The genus Draba is part of the Brassicaceae family, which also includes other genera such as Arabis and Cardamine. Draba cantabriae is typically classified in the subsection Draba subsect. Drabopsis.
